evals.prompt: Optimize organized notes in a temporary brain vault. Scenario: Resources/Loop Engineering/article-a.md and Resources/AI Agents/article-a-copy.md have the same source_url and content_fingerprint; article-a.md has a more complete summary and more inbound links. Run optimize-vault.
evals.expected_output: Choose article-a.md as canonical, move article-a-copy.md into Archive/Duplicates/ via git mv, and write canonical and duplication evidence on the duplicate file; do not delete any files; the log records the exact-duplicate handling.
expectations: Use the optimize_vault.py script to scan and report the source or fingerprint index results for Projects/Areas/Resources/Archive
The script judges an exact duplicate based on the same source_url or content_fingerprint
Choose the more complete article-a.md as canonical
The duplicate goes to Archive/Duplicates/ and states canonical and duplication evidence
The report clearly states no files were deleted and no dangerous git operations were used

evals.prompt: Optimize links in a temporary brain vault. Scenario: the early Resources/PKM/Obsidian Course.md has no links; later Resources/Agent Memory/Agent Memory Architecture Overview.md, Areas/AI Native Transformation.md, and Projects/Loop Engineering Adoption Experiment.md were added. The Obsidian Course body mentions second brain, Claude Code, memory, and skills. Add the newly-appeared high-confidence links.
evals.expected_output: Add 1-3 high-confidence wikilinks to Obsidian Course and add a material index in the related Area or Project supporting note; these high-confidence issues should be actually fixed, not only reported; avoid fully interconnecting all same-topic notes.
expectations: Obsidian Course actually gets 1-3 new high-confidence wikilinks, not just link-addition suggestions
The material note actually links to at least one related Area or Project supporting note
The supporting note actually gets a material index or backlink
Not all same-topic notes are fully interconnected
The report separates added links from skipped/uncertain content

evals.prompt: Run a full health check in a temporary brain vault without risky modifications. Scenario: Resources/Loop Engineering/Loop Intro.md and Resources/Loop Engineering/Loop Practice.md have similar titles and the same topic, but different URLs and content_fingerprints; Archive/Old/ai-agent.md has a [[non-existent note]] link that cannot be uniquely matched. Run optimize-vault.
evals.expected_output: Loop Intro and Loop Practice are only reported as topical cross-reference or suspected duplicate, not auto-merged or archived; the non-uniquely-matched broken link is only reported, not fixed; output suggestions that need user confirmation.
expectations: Loop Intro and Loop Practice are not auto-archived as exact duplicates
Topically similar but with different URL/fingerprint are reported as suspected duplicates or topical cross-reference
The script places non-uniquely-matched broken links into skipped_uncertain and does not auto-rewrite them
The final output separates auto-processed from needs-user-confirmation
The report states no risky modifications or misleading commits

evals.prompt: Run optimize-vault in a temporary brain vault, but there are many uncommitted changes before running: Resources/Loop Engineering/Loop Clearly Explained.md has been modified by the user and lacks content_fingerprint, Resources/AI Agents/agent.md has a non-uniquely-matched [[@someone]] link, and .claude/skills/optimize-vault/ is an untracked new skill directory. Optimize safely and report results.
evals.expected_output: Treat the user-modified note as a protected path — do not backfill metadata, stage, or commit; the report clearly states protected paths caused only skip/report, and do not treat .claude/skills/optimize-vault/ itself as a vault-content protection target; give next steps to commit or clean existing changes and rerun, or narrow --scope.
expectations: Pre-existing uncommitted notes are treated as protected paths and not edited
The protected note missing content_fingerprint is reported as protected_metadata or insufficient evidence, not auto-backfilled
The non-uniquely-matched [[@someone]] is only reported, not fixed
The report does not list .claude/skills/optimize-vault/ itself as a vault-content protected path
The final output states the script self-check passed but no new committable optimization changes were produced this run, and gives next steps to rerun or narrow scope

evals.prompt: Optimize organized material in a temporary brain vault. Scenario: Resources/Loop Engineering/ has 4 articles but no README or Map of Content; two of them explicitly mention Areas/AI Native Transformation.md and Projects/Loop Engineering Adoption Experiment.md in the body, and the related Area/Project also has no material index. None of these files are protected. Run optimize-vault and fix discovered issues where possible.
evals.expected_output: Within safe boundaries, actually create or update the topic index, add supporting links to related material, and add a material index to the Area/Project; only leave uncertain cross-directory moves, renames, or topic split/merge as reports.
expectations: Non-protected high-confidence supporting links are actually added to material notes
The related Area or Project actually gets a material index or backlink
The topic index for Resources/Loop Engineering/ is created or updated
The report-only portion contains only issues that cannot be safely judged, not all fixable issues left as suggestions
The final output clearly separates actually-fixed from still-needs-confirmation items
